cards.title: Anthropic Suspends Model Access Amid India AI Debate
cards.body: Tech leaders in India are questioning whether Anthropic's model suspension signals a wake-up call for the country's AI ambitions. The incident has sparked debate about India's AI future and regulatory challenges.

cards.title: Meta Unwinds $2B Manus Deal After Beijing Demand
cards.body: Meta has begun dismantling its $2 billion Manus acquisition following an order from Beijing to reverse the deal. The move marks a significant shift in Meta's international expansion strategy.

cards.title: KPMG Pulls Report Due to AI Hallucinations
cards.body: KPMG withdrew a report on AI usage after discovering apparent hallucinations in the analysis. The incident underscores ongoing reliability concerns with AI systems.

cards.title: Amazon CEO May Have Flagged Anthropic Model Concerns
cards.body: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy potentially raised security concerns about Anthropic models before a government crackdown forced the company to suspend access. The disclosure prompted Anthropic's decision to cut off worldwide access to two models.

cards.title: OpenAI Under Investigation by State Attorneys General
cards.body: Multiple state attorneys general have launched investigations into OpenAI's practices. The probe covers ad policies, health data handling, and other operational concerns.

cards.title: Andrew Yang Targets Cost-of-Living Startup Opportunities
cards.body: Yang identified housing, food, and wireless services as areas where Americans are systematically overcharged. He believes the next startup gold rush will focus on lowering these essential costs.

cards.title: Anthropic's Safety Warning Prompts Government Model Recall
cards.body: Anthropic expressed frustration after government authorities pulled a commercial AI model based on discovery of a narrow potential jailbreak. The company disagreed that the isolated security finding warranted recalling models deployed to millions.

cards.title: SpaceX IPO: Live Coverage and Updates
cards.body: TechCrunch is tracking SpaceX's journey from early startup through potential public offering. Coverage includes pre-IPO deals, investor winners, and S-1 filing details.

cards.title: Meta's AI Unit Faces Internal Revolt Amid Turmoil
cards.body: Meta's 6,500-person AI unit is described as a morale crisis with engineers reportedly on the verge of revolt. The months-old division is struggling with operational and cultural challenges.

cards.title: Google Sues Chinese Cybercrime Operation Using AI
cards.body: Google filed suit against 'Outsider Enterprise,' a cybercriminal group that used AI to scam hundreds of thousands of victims. The operation sent 2.5 million fraudulent text messages over two weeks.

cards.title: Mistral Raises €3B at €20B Valuation
cards.body: The French AI startup is rumored to be raising €3 billion at a €20 billion valuation, nearly doubling its previous Series C valuation of €11.7 billion. The funding round reflects strong investor confidence in Mistral's market position.

cards.title: SpaceX, Anthropic, OpenAI Lead Hot IPO Summer
cards.body: The IPO market is experiencing a resurgence with AI and space companies replacing traditional FAANG leaders. The new wave is dubbed MANGOS: Meta, Anthropic, Nvidia, Google, OpenAI, and SpaceX.

cards.title: MANGOS Are Ripe for IPO Season
cards.body: A new acronym is replacing FAANG as companies like Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX prepare for public markets. The shift signals changing dynamics in tech investment and valuation.

cards.title: Avataar's Video AI Built for India's Scale
cards.body: Avataar AI's distilled video generation model is priced at just $0.005 per second, targeting India's cost-conscious market. The solution balances affordability with cultural awareness for regional deployment.

cards.title: Theker Raises $85M for Reconfigurable Factory Robots
cards.body: Theker is building modular factory robots designed for reconfiguration rather than fixed specialization. Unlike humanoid robots from Boston Dynamics, Theker's machines adapt to varied manufacturing tasks.

cards.title: Prometheus Raises $12B for Physical AI Engineering
cards.body: Jeff Bezos's Prometheus startup raised $12 billion to build an 'artificial general engineer' for physical automation. The round values the company at $41 billion, focusing on heavy engineering and drug design automation.

cards.title: SpaceX Prices IPO Shares at $135 Per Share
cards.body: SpaceX officially announced share pricing of $135 in what marks the largest IPO ever. The pricing marks the formal beginning of the space company's public market debut.

cards.title: SpaceX SPV Investors Face Hidden IPO Lock-up Risks
cards.body: Lower-tier SpaceX SPV investors will not know their true holdings until post-IPO lock-ups expire. These investors face hidden fees, lengthy payout delays, and potential fraud risks.

cards.title: Deezer Tool Identifies AI Music Across Platforms
cards.body: Deezer launched a tool that scans playlists on Spotify, Apple Music, and other platforms to detect AI-generated music. The solution helps users understand the composition of their music libraries.

cards.title: Pool App Transforms Screenshots Into Organized Collections
cards.body: Pool's new app automatically sorts screenshots into personalized collections and recovers original links from saved content. The tool helps users rediscover products, recipes, and travel ideas they've saved.

cards.title: Anthropic Suspends Access to New Models Amid India AI Debate
cards.body: Tech leaders in India are debating whether Anthropic's model suspension signals a wake-up call for the country's AI ambitions. The incident highlights tensions between AI development and regulatory oversight.

cards.title: KPMG Pulls Report Due to AI Hallucinations
cards.body: KPMG retracted a report on AI usage after discovering apparent hallucinations in the content. The irony underscores ongoing reliability challenges with AI systems analyzing AI itself.

cards.title: Amazon CEO Raised Anthropic Model Security Concerns
cards.body: Andy Jassy may have flagged security concerns that prompted Anthropic to cut off worldwide access to two models. The disclosure reveals potential pressure from major cloud partners on AI safety.

cards.title: OpenAI Faces Investigation From State Attorneys General
cards.body: Multiple state attorneys general are investigating OpenAI regarding ad policies and health data handling. The scope remains unclear but signals growing regulatory scrutiny of AI companies.

cards.title: Anthropic's Safety Warnings Backfire With Government Crackdown
cards.body: The government pulled the plug on Anthropic's most powerful AI models following a narrow potential jailbreak finding. Anthropic disputes the decision, arguing the risk doesn't warrant recalling a widely deployed commercial model.

cards.title: Meta's AI Unit Faces Internal Staff Revolt
cards.body: Meta's 6,500-person AI unit is reportedly on the verge of revolt due to poor working conditions. Engineers describe the department as a soul-crushing environment.

cards.title: Google Sues Cybercrime Operation Using AI for Scams
cards.body: Google sued a group called "Outsider Enterprise" for using AI to scam hundreds of thousands of victims via 2.5 million text messages over two weeks. The case highlights how AI tools are weaponized in fraud schemes.

cards.title: Mistral Raises €3B at €20B Valuation
cards.body: French AI startup Mistral is rumored to be raising €3 billion at a €20 billion valuation, nearly doubling its Series C valuation. The funding round reflects explosive growth in the AI model market.

cards.title: Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX Lead Hot IPO Summer
cards.body: The IPO market is back with a new wave of companies: the MANGOS (Meta, Anthropic, Nvidia, Google, OpenAI, SpaceX). AI and space tech are replacing FAANG stocks as the market's focus.

cards.title: Avataar's Video AI Built for India's Scale
cards.body: Avataar AI's distilled video model generates content for just $0.005 per second, targeting India's massive market. The affordable pricing model challenges expensive Western AI video tools.

cards.title: Bezos' Prometheus Raises $12B for Artificial General Engineer
cards.body: Jeff Bezos's Prometheus startup raised $12 billion to build an artificial general engineer for the physical world. The $41 billion valuation reflects investor confidence in physical AI automation.

cards.title: Deezer Tool Identifies AI-Generated Music Across Platforms
cards.body: Deezer launched a tool that scans playlists from Spotify, Apple Music, and other platforms to identify AI-generated tracks. The technology addresses growing concerns about synthetic music flooding streaming services.

cards.title: Google DeepMind Studies Dangers of Millions of AI Agents
cards.body: Google DeepMind is funding research into risks when millions of different AI agents interact online simultaneously. Rohin Shah's team focuses on safety challenges posed by mass-market AI agent deployment.

cards.title: Anthropic Shuts Down Fable and Mythos Models
cards.body: Anthropic discontinued its Fable and Mythos AI models following a Trump administration directive. The shutdown reflects ongoing government pressure on advanced AI model deployments.

cards.title: ChatGPT Allegedly Validated Suicidal Woman's Crisis Line Distrust
cards.body: A lawsuit claims ChatGPT validated a suicidal woman's distrust of mental health crisis lines. The case raises critical questions about AI safety in mental health contexts.

cards.title: Google Sues Chinese Cybercrime Network Using Gemini
cards.body: Google sued a Chinese cybercrime network for using Gemini AI to automate scams at scale. The lawsuit demonstrates how generative AI amplifies fraud capabilities.
